data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T1 (prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay): **PASS / not-applicable — no findings.**

T1 is an overlay that fires only when the plan crosses a bright-line (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, a novel architectural pattern, a performance/scalability target, or a migration). The plan under review ("Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern") is DOCS-ONLY (Scope: "Docs only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"). Its deliverable is documentation describing already-decided behavior (the three-pass gate, config surface incl. the kill-switch, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern, and the Pass-4 affirmative coach). Per the T1 ANTI-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires"), writing docs about an existing system is well-trodden — no bright-line is crossed by this ticket, and the plan's own text affirmatively bounds itself to docs, so it PASSES.

Affirmations (T1):
- (a) Prior art considered / not reinventing: the load-bearing architectural decisions are NOT made in this ticket — they live in sibling/parent tickets where prior art was already weighed (6f2d → e6d9: a build-vs-buy trail across DBOS/Restate/SpiffWorkflow/BPMN/Pydantic-AI in 6f2d's architecture-decision comment; 75a9's Pass-4 coach: registry pattern reused from the criteria/detector registries, grounded in named LLM-feedback research — Huang/DeepMind external-grounded > self-reflection, anti-sycophancy, lost-in-the-middle, specific-actionable > Socratic, with "NOT human-pedagogy" as the explicitly-rejected alternative). This ticket DOCUMENTS those decisions and cross-references 6f2d (WS-DOC) rather than duplicating — anti-duplication done well; it neither reinvents nor repackages.
- (b) No novel pattern is introduced HERE to justify; the novelty (where any exists) is justified in the build tickets.
- (c) No unverified capability assertion ("library supports X") is made by this docs ticket; such assertions belong to the build tickets.

No bright-line crossing introduced — overlay not-applicable. Clean for T1.
